title: Group content by the user's job
statement: Combine controls and explanations that serve one user job; split genuinely separate capabilities into separately legible features.
rationale: Grouping by implementation or chronology obscures what users can accomplish and why related controls belong together.

prefer: Group controls, examples, and explanation around one coherent user job.
Separate capabilities when they have different goals or outcomes.
avoid: Grouping by implementation module when the user jobs differ.
Splitting one job into several arbitrary feature fragments.

when: A page or document describes multiple controls or capabilities.
not_when: Side-by-side comparison of true peer capabilities is the job.

evidence.summary: Asked releases to split a combined capability into separately named features that match distinct user jobs.
Asked dragging and docking to be one feature, while unrelated formula and tab improvements remain separate groups.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Activated only for the owner-requested initial bootstrap because repeated independent direct instructions or corrections establish this decision boundary.

positive_examples: Group controls, examples, and explanation around one coherent user job.
Separate capabilities when they have different goals or outcomes.
negative_examples: Grouping by implementation module when the user jobs differ.
Splitting one job into several arbitrary feature fragments.

checks: Can each group be named as one user job?
Do controls in the group share an outcome?
